body: ## Problem When pasting text into a Neovim terminal buffer (`:terminal`) running a program that uses bracketed paste mode (e.g. Claude Code, or any TUI relying on `\e[200~`), the child process never receives the bracketed paste escape sequences (`\e[200~` / `\e[201~`). This causes garbled/interleaved output for large pastes, because the child process cannot distinguish pasted text from typed input. **Pasting directly into the terminal emulator (e.g. Ghostty) works correctly** — the issue is specifically in the Neovim terminal buffer layer. ## Steps to reproduce 1. Open Neovim: `nvim --clean` 2. Open a terminal buffer: `:terminal` 3. Start a program that uses bracketed paste (e.g. Claude Code, or any TUI that relies on `\e[200~`) 4. Paste a large block of text (e.g. a multi-line JSON string) via `Cmd+V` / `Ctrl+Shift+V` 5. **Expected**: Program receives bracketed paste and handles it atomically (e.g. Claude Code shows `[Pasted text #n +x lines]`) 6. **Actual**: Program receives raw characters without bracketed paste framing, resulting in garbled/interleaved output ## Root cause In `src/nvim/terminal.c`, the `terminal_paste()` function (line ~1285) calls `vterm_keyboard_start_paste()` and `vterm_keyboard_end_paste()`: ```c void terminal_paste(int count, String *y_array, size_t y_size) { if (y_size == 0) { return; } vterm_keyboard_start_paste(curbuf->terminal->vt); // <-- writes to vterm OUTPUT buffer // ... loop sending text via terminal_send() which writes to the PTY ... vterm_keyboard_end_paste(curbuf->terminal->vt); // <-- writes to vterm OUTPUT buffer } ``` However, `vterm_keyboard_start_paste()` and `vterm_keyboard_end_paste()` (in `src/nvim/vterm/keyboard.c`) push the escape sequences to **libvterm's internal output buffer** (display side), not to the PTY input stream: ```c void vterm_keyboard_start_paste(VTerm *vt) { if (vt->state->mode.bracketpaste) { vterm_push_output_sprintf_ctrl(vt, C1_CSI, "200~"); // output buffer, not PTY } } ``` The actual text is sent to the PTY via `terminal_send()`, but the bracketed paste framing goes to the wrong destination. The child process receives the text without `\e[200~...\e[201~` wrapping. ## Suggested fix Replace `vterm_keyboard_start/end_paste()` calls with direct `terminal_send()` calls, while still respecting the child process's `bracketpaste` mode: ```c void terminal_paste(int count, String *y_array, size_t y_size) { if (y_size == 0) { return; } if (curbuf->terminal->vt->state->mode.bracketpaste) { terminal_send(curbuf->terminal, "\x1b[200~", 6); } // ... existing send loop unchanged ... if (curbuf->terminal->vt->state->mode.bracketpaste) { terminal_send(curbuf->terminal, "\x1b[201~", 6); } } ``` ## Lua workaround Until this is fixed upstream, users can work around the issue by overriding `vim.paste()` in their config: ```lua local orig_paste = vim.paste vim.paste = function(lines, phase) if vim.bo.buftype == "terminal" then local chan = vim.b.terminal_job_id if chan then if phase == 1 or phase == -1 then vim.api.nvim_chan_send(chan, "\x1b[200~") end vim.api.nvim_chan_send(chan, table.concat(lines, "\r")) if phase == 3 or phase == -1 then vim.api.nvim_chan_send(chan, "\x1b[201~") end return true end end return orig_paste(lines, phase) end ``` ## Environment - Neovim 0.12.1 - Terminal: Ghostty 1.3.1 - OS: macOS - My neovim config: https://github.com/fredrikaverpil/dotfiles/tree/main/nvim-fredrik

comment_data.body: Please try nightly: the [Releases](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/releases/tag/nightly) page has pre-built archives for Linux/Windows/macOS. Check `:version` to confirm the version.

Unfortunately the issue remains for me in nightly (installed via bob):

```
NVIM v0.13.0-dev-205+g0e07b2a1e2
Build type: RelWithDebInfo
LuaJIT 2.1.1774896198
Vim versions: 8.1, 8.2, 9.0, 9.1, 9.2
Compilation: /usr/bin/cc -O2 -g -DRELDEBUG -flto=thin -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -Wno-unused-parameter -Wstrict-prototypes -std=gnu99 -Wshadow -Wconversion -Wvla -Wdouble-promotion -Wmissing-noreturn -Wmissing-format-attribute -Wmissing-prototypes -Wno-unused-function -fsigned-char -fstack-protector-strong -Wimplicit-fallthrough -fdiagnostics-color=always -Wl,-export_dynamic -DHAVE_UNIBILIUM -DUNIT_TESTING -D_GNU_SOURCE -DUTF8PROC_STATIC -I/Users/runner/work/neovim/neovim/.deps/usr/include/luajit-2.1 -I/Users/runner/work/neovim/neovim/.deps/usr/include -I/Users/runner/work/neovim/neovim/build/src/nvim/auto -I/Users/runner/work/neovim/neovim/build/include -I/Users/runner/work/neovim/neovim/build/cmake.config -I/Users/runner/work/neovim/neovim/src -I/Applications/Xcode_15.4.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk/usr/include 

   system vimrc file: "$VIM/sysinit.vim"
  fall-back for $VIM: "/usr/local/share/nvim"

Run :checkhealth for more info
```

The lua workaround I posted does the trick though.

I went back and forth with Claude Opus 4.6 on this and it came out with a revised suggested fix:

> **Corrected Analysis**
>
> The original analysis about vterm's output buffer was wrong (as zeertzjq pointed out — Neovim registers an output callback that routes vterm output through `terminal_send()`).
>
> **Actual root cause**
>
> The default vim.paste() Lua handler (in runtime/lua/vim/_core/editor.lua) processes streamed paste in chunks (~4KB each, split by the TUI's KEY_BUFFER_SIZE). For terminal buffers, each chunk calls:
> 
> `vim.paste(lines, phase) → nvim_put(lines, 'c') → do_put() → terminal_paste()`
>
> `terminal_paste()` calls `vterm_keyboard_start_paste()` and `vterm_keyboard_end_paste()` around each chunk. This means each chunk gets its own `\e[200~...\e[201~` framing:
>
> `\e[200~chunk1\e[201~\e[200~chunk2\e[201~\e[200~chunk3\e[201~...`
>
> For the test JSON (~3KB), this produced 52 separate bracketed paste events in rapid succession. Between each `\e[201~` and the next `\e[200~`, the child process briefly exits paste mode, processes/renders output, then re-enters — causing display interleaving and garbled output.
>
> **How we confirmed this**
>
> We ran a series of A/B tests, all using `nvim_chan_send()` (bypassing nvim_put/terminal_paste entirely) to isolate the variable:
>
>
> ```
> ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┬───────────────────────┬─────────┐
> │                    Test                    │        Framing        │     Text delivery     │ Result  │
> ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────┤
> │ Single \e[200~...\e[201~ around all chunks │ single pair           │ single blob, \r sep   │ works   │
> ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────┤
> │ Single pair, \n separators                 │ single pair           │ single blob, \n sep   │ works   │
> ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────┤
> │ Single pair, per-line writes               │ single pair           │ one write per line    │ works   │
> ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────┤
> │ Per-chunk \e[200~...\e[201~ (~52 pairs)    │ per-chunk pairs       │ single blob per chunk │ garbled │
> ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────┤
> │ Default vim.paste → nvim_put path          │ per-chunk (via vterm) │ terminal_paste()      │ garbled │
> └────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┴───────────────────────┴─────────┘
> ```
>
> The per-chunk framing test confirmed the garbling with zero involvement from nvim_put/terminal_paste — just nvim_chan_send with per-chunk \e[200~...\e[201~ wrapping was enough to reproduce it.
>
> **The fix**
>
> vim.paste() should handle terminal buffers at the Lua level by sending a single \e[200~ at phase 1 and a single \e[201~ at phase 3, with raw text for phase 2 chunks — rather than delegating to nvim_put() → terminal_paste() which wraps each chunk independently.

But I cannot vouch for Claude here. I did not dig into this myself.

Hopefully, Claudes input here is useful. Again, I cannot vouch for it...

> The cleanest fix is in runtime/lua/vim/_core/editor.lua — handle terminal buffers directly in vim.paste() instead of routing through nvim_put() → terminal_paste(). Here's what the diff would look like:
>
> ```lua
> -- Current code (line ~185):
> if mode:find('^i') or mode:find('^n?t') then -- Insert mode or Terminal buffer
>   vim.api.nvim_put(lines, 'c', false, true)
>
> -- Fixed:
> if mode:find('^n?t') then -- Terminal buffer
>   local chan = vim.b.terminal_job_id
>   if chan then
>     if is_first_chunk then
>       vim.api.nvim_chan_send(chan, '\x1b[200~')
>     end
>     vim.api.nvim_chan_send(chan, table.concat(lines, '\r'))
>     if is_last_chunk then
>       vim.api.nvim_chan_send(chan, '\x1b[201~')
>     end
>   end
> elseif mode:find('^i') then -- Insert mode
>   vim.api.nvim_put(lines, 'c', false, true)
> ```
>
> The key insight: is_first_chunk and is_last_chunk are already computed at the top of vim.paste() (lines 155-156). The fix just uses them to send a single bracketed paste pair around the entire stream, instead of letting each chunk independently wrap itself.
> 
> An alternative C-level approach would be to split terminal_paste() into session-aware functions (start/data/end) and have vim.paste() call them at the right phases. But the Lua fix is simpler and self-contained — it avoids the nvim_put → do_put → terminal_paste path entirely for terminal buffers, which is unnecessary overhead anyway since all it does is write to the PTY.
Hmm, this solution sends bracket paste sequences even if the terminal program doesn't support them. I think sending the bracket paste sequences still needs to be in C, perhaps in `nvim_paste()`.
This is also another problem that this doesn't work for terminal buffers opened by `nvim_open_term`. In that case, the paste should go to the `on_input` callback.
Many thanks @zeertzjq and @justinmk - works great now in nightly! ❤️

title: terminal: bracketed paste sequences not forwarded to child process PTY
